---
title: "HW 1 — Setup and your first Quarto document"
subtitle: "The first artifact in your portfolio"
toc: true
---

> **Pairs with:** [Lab 1 — Install the stack](../labs/lab01-install-stack.qmd).
> Due date and submission link live in Blackboard.

The goal of this assignment is small but real: get the course's
software stack working on your computer and **render a Quarto
document** that contains an equation and a small R computation.

This is the first artifact in your portfolio.

## What to submit

Two files, both produced from the same source:

1. `hw01.qmd` — your Quarto source file.
2. `hw01.pdf` — the rendered PDF output.

Submit both to Blackboard.

**File naming.** Use exactly: `Lastname_First_hw01.qmd` and
`Lastname_First_hw01.pdf`.

## Requirements

| # | Requirement | Points |
|---|-------------|--------|
| 1 | The `.qmd` file renders cleanly to PDF with no errors. | 25 |
| 2 | The PDF contains a **title block** with your name. | 10 |
| 3 | The PDF includes **at least one displayed equation** (between `$$...$$`). | 15 |
| 4 | The PDF includes **at least one R code chunk** that runs and shows output. | 20 |
| 5 | The PDF includes an **AI Use Note** (one or two sentences) stating whether AI assistance was used and, if so, what for. | 10 |
| 6 | Your portfolio folder follows the suggested structure (`math-software-portfolio/hw01/`). | 10 |
| 7 | Your submitted source file is the source of the submitted PDF (no last-minute manual PDF edits). | 10 |

Total: 100.

## Step-by-step

If you haven't already, work through
[Lab 1 — Install the stack](../labs/lab01-install-stack.qmd) first.
The minimal `.qmd` shown there is enough to satisfy this assignment;
you are welcome to extend it.

1. Install R, RStudio, TinyTeX, Quarto (and optionally Git).
2. Create your `math-software-portfolio/hw01/` folder.
3. Create `hw01.qmd` and put your **name** in the front matter.
4. Add a displayed equation (the quadratic formula, the Euler
   identity, anything).
5. Add an R chunk that does something simple and shows output (e.g.,
   `mean(rnorm(10))` with `set.seed(1)`).
6. Add a short **AI Use Note**.
7. Run `quarto render hw01.qmd` and confirm you get a clean PDF.
8. Submit both files to Blackboard.

## AI use

You may use an AI assistant to help with setup, troubleshooting, or
syntax. Include a one- or two-sentence **AI Use Note** in your
submission. See [resources/ai-use-guidelines.qmd](../resources/ai-use-guidelines.qmd)
for what a good note looks like.

## A reminder on portfolio location

Your portfolio can live in **a local folder, a private repository, or
a public repository if you choose**. Public posting is encouraged
when appropriate but **not required**. Blackboard remains the
official submission inbox.
